sourceDocuments.title: DOC 1 — FY25 Q3 P&L Summary and Forecast
sourceDocuments.body:
**Q3 FY25 Actuals vs. Plan (prepared by VP Finance)**

| Metric | Q3 Actual | Q3 Plan | YoY |
|---|---|---|---|
| ARR (ending) | $62.1M | $63.5M | +28% |
| Net New ARR | $3.2M | $4.1M | -22% |
| Revenue | $14.8M | $15.0M | +26% |
| Gross Margin | 71.4% | 74.0% | -310 bps |
| S&M % of Revenue | 34% | 32% | +200 bps |
| R&D % of Revenue | 22% | 21% | flat |
| Operating Margin | 16.2% | 19.0% | -280 bps |
| FCF | $2.1M | $2.6M | -- |

**Commentary:** Gross margin compression driven primarily by ChainSight acquisition (closed Q1 FY25) — their hosting stack runs at 58% GM and migration to our infra is now estimated to complete Q2 FY26 (was Q4 FY25). Net new ARR miss concentrated in enterprise segment ($1.4M of the $0.9M shortfall). Mid-market and the new F&B vertical are tracking ahead. Cash position $48M; no debt. VP Finance recommends pulling FY25 exit ARR guidance from $68M to $66.5M.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 2 — Pipeline Review Deck (CRO, Week 38)
sourceDocuments.body:
**Pipeline Snapshot — entering Q4 FY25**

- Total qualified pipeline: $41M (coverage 3.4x against $12M Q4 net new target)
- Enterprise (>$250K ACV): $18M, down from $24M entering Q3. Win rate slipped from 27% to 19% over last two quarters. Three deals >$1M slipped from Q3 to Q4; one ($1.8M, automotive Tier 1) lost to incumbent ERP add-on.
- Mid-market: $16M, healthy, win rate stable at 32%.
- F&B vertical (new GTM motion launched Q1): $7M pipeline from a standing start. Win rate 41% on closed deals (n=11). Two reference customers (a regional dairy co-op and a specialty bakery chain) generating strong inbound.

**CRO commentary:** "Enterprise softness is real and I think structural for 2 more quarters — buyers are consolidating onto ERP-adjacent suites. F&B is the bright spot; if we doubled SDR headcount there in Q1 FY26 I think we could 3x that pipeline. I'd reallocate from enterprise outbound, not add net new heads."

**Risk flag:** Top 10 enterprise renewals in H1 FY26 represent $14.2M ARR. Two are in active RFPs.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 3 — Net Retention & Churn Cohort Analysis
sourceDocuments.body:
**NRR / GRR by segment (TTM, prepared by RevOps)**

| Segment | NRR | GRR | Logo Churn |
|---|---|---|---|
| Enterprise | 108% | 91% | 6.2% |
| Mid-market | 116% | 94% | 7.8% |
| ChainSight installed base | 94% | 86% | 11.4% |
| F&B (early) | n/m | 100% | 0% |
| **Blended** | **109%** | **92%** | **7.6%** |

**Key findings:**
1. Blended NRR has fallen from 121% (FY24) to 109% — driven roughly equally by (a) ChainSight base attrition and (b) reduced expansion in enterprise (fewer seat adds, deals to add modules pushed).
2. ChainSight cohort: of 84 inherited customers, 12 churned in first 12 months post-close vs. 6 modeled. Exit interviews cite product roadmap uncertainty and a 14% list-price harmonization done in Q2.
3. Mid-market expansion motion (usage-based pricing pilot) showing 124% NRR on the 60-account pilot — RevOps recommends GA in Q1 FY26.
4. F&B: too early but every account has expanded users in first 90 days.

**RevOps recommendation:** Freeze further price harmonization on ChainSight base; invest in a dedicated CSM pod ($1.2M annualized) to stabilize.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 4 — Engineering Capacity & Integration Status
sourceDocuments.body:
**Memo from CTO to ELT — Integration & Roadmap Capacity**

Current eng headcount: 142 FTE (38 inherited from ChainSight, 12 from prior Velocity acquisition fully integrated).

**Capacity allocation FY25 actual:**
- ChainSight platform migration: 34% of capacity (planned 22%)
- Core platform / roadmap: 41% (planned 55%)
- F&B vertical features (lot traceability, FSMA 204): 9%
- Keep-the-lights-on / security: 16%

**Issues:**
- Migration overrun is the single biggest drag on roadmap. We are 2 quarters behind on the unified data model. Every quarter of delay costs us ~$400K in duplicate hosting + ~$2M in deferred cross-sell readiness.
- F&B momentum has outpaced our roadmap commitments. To close the 6 deals currently in late-stage, we need FSMA 204 compliance features by March 2026 — currently scoped for Sept 2026.
- Attrition in ChainSight eng team running at 22% annualized; key architect departed in August.

**Ask:** Either (a) approve 15 net new senior eng hires ($3.6M loaded) to parallelize, or (b) explicitly deprioritize one of: ChainSight migration, F&B acceleration, or core roadmap velocity. Cannot do all three on current headcount.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 5 — Board Pre-Read Notes from Lead Director
sourceDocuments.body:
**Email from Lead Independent Director, sent to CEO + CFO**

"Ahead of the offsite, a few framing thoughts from my conversations with the other directors:

1. **Wall Street comp set.** Our public comparables (think Samsara, Procore at similar scale) are being rewarded for the Rule of 40 with a *growth* tilt, not margin tilt. Trading at 8-11x ARR with 30%+ growth, vs. 4-6x for sub-25% growers. We are drifting toward the second bucket. The board does not want to optimize for short-term margin if it means the multiple compresses further before a 2027 liquidity event.

2. **M&A indigestion is a real concern.** Two directors raised whether ChainSight has been value-destructive on a risk-adjusted basis. I'd like to see a clear-eyed assessment in your scenarios.

3. **Capital deployment.** You have $48M cash and are FCF positive. The default of 'keep accumulating cash' is not acceptable to this board. Either deploy it (organic reinvestment, third tuck-in, buyback) or articulate why holding it is the right call.

4. We want three scenarios with crisp tradeoffs, not a single recommendation dressed up as options. Show us what you'd actually NOT do in each case."

— Lead Director

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 6 — Competitor & Market Intel Brief
sourceDocuments.body:
**Prepared by Head of Strategy / Corp Dev — September**

**Competitive landscape shifts (last 90 days):**

- **FlowChain (private, ~$90M ARR):** Raised $120M Series E in August at $1.4B valuation. Publicly stated they will "invest aggressively in F&B and pharma verticals." Hired our former VP Product (left Lattica in June).
- **OracleSCM / SAP IBP:** Both bundling lightweight visibility modules into ERP renewals at near-zero incremental cost. This is the structural enterprise headwind the CRO referenced. We estimate this affects ~$8-10M of our enterprise pipeline annually.
- **Veridian Systems (private, ~$25M ARR, F&B-focused):** Approached us informally in July about a sale. Asking ~$110M (4.4x ARR). Strong FSMA 204 product, ~140 F&B logos including 4 of the top 20 US food processors. 18% growth, breakeven.
- **Two smaller players (combined ~$15M ARR)** in distress; could be tuck-ins at <2x ARR.

**Strategic implication:** F&B window is open for ~12-18 months before FlowChain or an ERP incumbent locks it down. Veridian acquisition would compress our F&B GTM timeline by ~24 months but consume $100M+ of capital (would require debt or secondary).

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 7 — FY26 Operating Model Sensitivities
sourceDocuments.body:
**FP&A Sensitivity Workbook — three working scenarios**

All figures FY26 (year ending Jan 2027). Starting ARR $66.5M.

| | Defensive | Balanced | Reaccelerate |
|---|---|---|---|
| Net new ARR | $14M | $20M | $28M |
| Exit ARR | $79M | $84M | $91M |
| YoY ARR growth | 19% | 26% | 37% |
| S&M spend | $26M (flat) | $32M | $42M |
| R&D spend | $19M | $23M | $28M |
| Op margin | 22% | 14% | 2% |
| FCF | +$13M | +$5M | -$4M |
| Year-end cash | $61M | $53M | $44M |
| Headcount delta | -3% | +12% | +28% |
| Rule of 40 | 41 | 40 | 39 |

**Embedded assumptions:**
- Defensive: no Veridian deal, ChainSight CSM pod funded, no F&B SDR expansion, migration finished Q2.
- Balanced: F&B SDR doubling, 8 net new eng hires, mid-market UBP GA, no acquisition, $10M ASR buyback authorized.
- Reaccelerate: Veridian acquisition ($110M, $60M cash + $50M seller note + secondary), full F&B build-out, FSMA 204 pulled to March, 22 net new eng hires.

**FP&A note:** All three clear Rule of 40, but the market is paying for the growth component, not the sum. Reaccelerate breaches our covenant-light minimum cash floor ($35M) only under a -15% revenue stress.

question: Synthesize a board-ready scenario memo for the FY26 offsite that (a) evaluates the Defensive, Balanced, and Reaccelerate cases against the Lead Director's framing on multiple compression and capital deployment, (b) takes a position on the Veridian acquisition and the ChainSight integration overrun, and (c) specifies what we would explicitly NOT do under each scenario and which one you recommend, with the capital-efficiency justification.

personaText:
Persona: COO at Lumenstack, a Series B B2B SaaS company providing observability tooling for mid-market engineering teams. We closed a $32M Series B nine months ago, are at $14M ARR growing 60% YoY, burning ~$1.3M/month with ~14 months of runway. I own operations, hiring, vendor spend, and cross-functional execution under pressure from the board to extend runway while protecting growth.

Scenario: The board has asked for a scenario plan ahead of the Q1 offsite covering three operating modes (accelerate, steady, conserve) given softening pipeline signals, an aggressive new entrant, and rising infra costs. I need to synthesize signals from finance, GTM, product, and people functions into a defensible scenario plan with trigger points and pre-committed actions before the offsite in two weeks.
scenario: The board has asked for a scenario plan ahead of the Q1 offsite covering three operating modes (accelerate, steady, conserve) given softening pipeline signals, an aggressive new entrant, and rising infra costs. I need to synthesize signals from finance, GTM, product, and people functions into a defensible scenario plan with trigger points and pre-committed actions before the offsite in two weeks.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 1 — Q4 Runway Model & P&L Summary (from VP Finance)
sourceDocuments.body:
**Runway Model — as of Dec 31**

- Cash on hand: $18.2M
- Net burn (Q4 avg): $1.34M/month → 13.6 months runway at current pace
- ARR: $14.1M (+58% YoY); Q4 net new ARR $1.6M (vs. $2.1M plan)
- Gross margin: 71% (down from 74% in Q2 — infra cost spike)
- Magic Number: 0.7 (was 1.1 in H1)
- CAC payback: 22 months (target <18)

**Scenarios modeled:**
| Scenario | Monthly Burn | Runway | FY ARR Exit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Accelerate (hire +25 FTE) | $1.7M | 10.7mo | $24M |
| Steady (hold headcount) | $1.3M | 14.0mo | $21M |
| Conserve (RIF 12%, freeze) | $0.85M | 21.4mo | $18M |

**VP Finance commentary:** "Magic number deterioration is the real flag. We're spending more to acquire less. Recommend conserve unless Q1 pipeline coverage clears 3.5x by Feb 15. Board will not approve another raise at flat metrics."

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 2 — Pipeline Review & GTM Health (VP Sales)
sourceDocuments.body:
**Q1 Pipeline Snapshot — Jan 8**

- Q1 quota: $5.2M new ARR
- Pipeline coverage: 2.8x (target 4x)
- Win rate trailing 90d: 19% (was 27% in Q2)
- Avg sales cycle: 84 days (up from 61)
- Deals lost to "ObserveIQ" (new entrant): 9 of last 22 losses cited them

**Segment performance:**
- Mid-market (core): coverage 3.1x, win rate 22%
- Enterprise (expansion bet): coverage 1.9x, win rate 11%, 3 reps ramping

**VP Sales note:** "Enterprise motion isn't working yet — reps need 9-12mo ramp and we're 5mo in. Mid-market is healthy but ObserveIQ is undercutting on price by 30-40%. I need 4 more mid-market AEs by March or we miss Q2. Cutting now = giving up the segment."

**Forecast:** Commit $3.1M, Best Case $4.4M (vs $5.2M quota)

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 3 — Net Revenue Retention & Churn Analysis (Customer Success Lead)
sourceDocuments.body:
**NRR Cohort Report — December**

- Gross retention: 89% (was 93% LY)
- Net retention: 108% (was 121% LY)
- Logo churn Q4: 6 customers ($780K ARR) — 4 cited "cost optimization," 2 moved to ObserveIQ
- Expansion ARR: $1.9M (down from $2.6M LY)

**Top churn risks (next 90 days):** $2.1M ARR across 11 accounts flagged red
- 7 accounts: budget cuts at customer
- 3 accounts: feature gaps vs ObserveIQ (distributed tracing depth)
- 1 account: implementation never completed

**CS Lead commentary:** "Our CSM:account ratio is 1:42 — industry benchmark is 1:25 for our ACV. We're reactive, not proactive. Two of the four cost-cited churns I believe we could have saved with earlier intervention. If we cut CS headcount in a conservation scenario, expect GRR to drop another 3-4 points."

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 4 — Engineering Velocity & Infra Cost Report (VP Engineering)
sourceDocuments.body:
**Eng Org Snapshot**

- Headcount: 47 (38 IC, 9 mgmt) — 32% of total FTE
- Q4 velocity: shipped 3 of 5 roadmap commitments
- Distributed tracing v2 (competitive gap closer): 60% complete, est. April GA
- Infra spend: $312K/month (was $198K/month in Q2) — driven by customer log volume

**Cost optimization opportunities identified:**
- Tiered storage migration: -$70K/mo, 8 weeks eng work
- Query engine rewrite: -$45K/mo, 14 weeks
- Multi-region consolidation: -$30K/mo but increases latency for EU customers

**VP Eng note:** "I can free up ~$140K/month in infra by Q3 if I dedicate 4 engineers to cost work. That trades against tracing v2 timeline — slips to June. Also: I'm losing 2 senior ICs to a competitor next month. Backfill in this market is 4-5 months and 15% comp inflation."

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 5 — Competitive Intel Brief: ObserveIQ (Product Marketing)
sourceDocuments.body:
**ObserveIQ — Threat Assessment**

- Founded 2022, raised $45M Series A in Sept (Sequoia-led)
- Pricing: ~35% below us on comparable workloads; aggressive 2-yr discounts
- Product: superior distributed tracing; weaker on alerting/dashboards
- Headcount: ~80, hiring 30+ in GTM per LinkedIn
- Customer poaching playbook: free 90-day migration + dedicated SE

**Where they're winning:** dev-tools-first buyers, <500 employee co's, technical evaluations
**Where we still win:** SOC2/compliance-driven buyers, enterprise procurement, integrated platform story

**PMM commentary:** "They are not better — they're cheaper and louder. Their funding gives them 18-24 months of aggressive pricing runway. We either match on price (margin death), differentiate on enterprise/compliance (slower deals), or accelerate tracing v2 to neutralize their wedge. Doing none of these = continued share loss in mid-market."

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 6 — People Ops: Retention, Comp, & Hiring Pipeline (VP People)
sourceDocuments.body:
**People Snapshot — January**

- Total FTE: 147 | Open reqs: 18 (12 GTM, 4 Eng, 2 G&A)
- Voluntary attrition trailing 6mo: 14% annualized (was 8%)
- Engagement score: 6.9/10 (down from 7.6) — top concerns: "strategic clarity," "compensation"
- 23 employees below market band per Q4 comp study; cost to true-up: $680K annualized

**Flight risk (mgr-flagged):** 11 employees, 6 in engineering, 3 in sales

**RIF scenario modeling (12% reduction = 18 roles):**
- Severance cost: ~$520K one-time
- Morale impact: "severe" — prior RIF at last company drove 25% additional voluntary attrition in 6mo
- Recommended cuts if forced: 4 G&A, 6 unfilled reqs, 5 underperformers, 3 strategic

**VP People note:** "A RIF without a clear narrative will accelerate the attrition we're already seeing. If we go conserve, we need a story employees believe."

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 7 — Board Pre-Read Notes & Investor Signals (CEO forwarded)
sourceDocuments.body:
**From Lead Investor (Series B partner) — email Jan 5:**

"Going into the Q1 board meeting, the partnership wants to see a credible path to default-alive without another round, OR a clear thesis on why doubling down now wins the category. Middle-of-the-road plans are what we're worried about. Specifically:

1. What is your trigger framework? Under what conditions do you cut vs. invest?
2. How do you think about ObserveIQ — are we in a knife fight or a different game?
3. The 22-month CAC payback is not financeable in this market. What's the plan to get under 18 by Q3?
4. We'd rather you cut hard now and re-accelerate in H2 than slow-bleed."

**CEO annotation:** "COO — I want your scenario plan to directly answer these four questions with pre-committed triggers, not just narrative. Don't bring three options of equal weight — bring a recommendation. Board is March 6."

**Other signals:** Two other portfolio cos in same fund did 15-20% RIFs in Q4. Tier-1 VC sentiment for infra/devtools cooling.

question: Produce a scenario plan memo for the March 6 board covering accelerate/steady/conserve modes — including a recommended path with justification, quantitative trigger conditions for switching modes, and the specific operational pre-commitments (hiring, infra, CS, comp, RIF design) required under each — that directly addresses the lead investor's four questions.

**VP Eng note:** "I can free up ~$140K/month in infra by Q3 if I dedicate 4 engineers to cost work. That trades against tracing v2 timeline — slips to June. Also: I'm losing 2 senior ICs to a competitor next month. Backfill in this market is 4-5 months and 15% comp inflation."


---

## DOC 5 — Competitive Intel Brief: ObserveIQ (Product Marketing)

**ObserveIQ — Threat Assessment**

- Founded 2022, raised $45M Series A in Sept (Sequoia-led)
- Pricing: ~35% below us on comparable workloads; aggressive 2-yr discounts
- Product: superior distributed tracing; weaker on alerting/dashboards
- Headcount: ~80, hiring 30+ in GTM per LinkedIn
- Customer poaching playbook: free 90-day migration + dedicated SE

**Where they're winning:** dev-tools-first buyers, <500 employee co's, technical evaluations
**Where we still win:** SOC2/compliance-driven buyers, enterprise procurement, integrated platform story

**PMM commentary:** "They are not better — they're cheaper and louder. Their funding gives them 18-24 months of aggressive pricing runway. We either match on price (margin death), differentiate on enterprise/compliance (slower deals), or accelerate tracing v2 to neutralize their wedge. Doing none of these = continued share loss in mid-market."


---

## DOC 6 — People Ops: Retention, Comp, & Hiring Pipeline (VP People)

**People Snapshot — January**

- Total FTE: 147 | Open reqs: 18 (12 GTM, 4 Eng, 2 G&A)
- Voluntary attrition trailing 6mo: 14% annualized (was 8%)
- Engagement score: 6.9/10 (down from 7.6) — top concerns: "strategic clarity," "compensation"
- 23 employees below market band per Q4 comp study; cost to true-up: $680K annualized

**Flight risk (mgr-flagged):** 11 employees, 6 in engineering, 3 in sales

**RIF scenario modeling (12% reduction = 18 roles):**
- Severance cost: ~$520K one-time
- Morale impact: "severe" — prior RIF at last company drove 25% additional voluntary attrition in 6mo
- Recommended cuts if forced: 4 G&A, 6 unfilled reqs, 5 underperformers, 3 strategic

**VP People note:** "A RIF without a clear narrative will accelerate the attrition we're already seeing. If we go conserve, we need a story employees believe."


---

## DOC 7 — Board Pre-Read Notes & Investor Signals (CEO forwarded)

**From Lead Investor (Series B partner) — email Jan 5:**

"Going into the Q1 board meeting, the partnership wants to see a credible path to default-alive without another round, OR a clear thesis on why doubling down now wins the category. Middle-of-the-road plans are what we're worried about. Specifically:

1. What is your trigger framework? Under what conditions do you cut vs. invest?
2. How do you think about ObserveIQ — are we in a knife fight or a different game?
3. The 22-month CAC payback is not financeable in this market. What's the plan to get under 18 by Q3?
4. We'd rather you cut hard now and re-accelerate in H2 than slow-bleed."

**CEO annotation:** "COO — I want your scenario plan to directly answer these four questions with pre-committed triggers, not just narrative. Don't bring three options of equal weight — bring a recommendation. Board is March 6."

**Other signals:** Two other portfolio cos in same fund did 15-20% RIFs in Q4. Tier-1 VC sentiment for infra/devtools cooling.

personaText:
Persona: COO of Lattica, a $62M ARR B2B SaaS platform providing workflow automation for mid-market finance teams. The company reached GAAP profitability six quarters ago, runs at 19% operating margin, and recently absorbed a $14M tuck-in acquisition (a reconciliation tool) that closed eight months ago. The COO owns operations, post-sales, and people functions, and partners with the CEO on go-to-market efficiency.

Scenario: The board has asked the executive team to present a 2026 operating scenario plan at the November offsite, with three planning cases (defend-margin, balanced, reaccelerate-growth). Wall Street comparables have punished growth-decel stories and the CEO is privately leaning toward a reaccelerate posture, but integration debt from the acquisition and rising attrition in the Customer Success org are surfacing operational risk. The COO must synthesize cross-functional inputs into a defensible scenario recommendation before the offsite.

Wall Street comparables have punished growth-decel stories and the CEO is privately leaning toward a reaccelerate posture, but integration debt from the acquisition and rising attrition in the Customer Success org are surfacing operational risk. The COO must synthesize cross-functional inputs into a defensible scenario recommendation before the offsite.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 1 — Q3 P&L Summary and FY26 Baseline
sourceDocuments.body:
**Prepared by:** VP Finance | **Date:** Oct 14

**Q3 Actuals**
- Revenue: $16.4M (Q3), ARR exit $62.1M
- YoY ARR growth: 17% (down from 24% prior year)
- Gross margin: 78% (flat)
- Operating margin: 19.2% ($3.15M op income)
- FCF: $4.1M; cash on BS: $71M

**FY26 Baseline (no strategy change)**
- Projected ARR growth: 14-15%
- Projected op margin: 20-21% (modest leverage from S&M efficiency)
- Headcount plan: +38 net (412 → 450)

**Commentary:** Sales efficiency has improved (Magic Number 0.9 → 1.1) but new logo ACV is softening. The acquired Reconcile.io product line is contributing $6.8M ARR but at 71% GM due to hosting overhang. To hit Rule of 40 above 35, we need either growth reacceleration or another 200bps of margin. Both are hard from here without investment shifts.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 2 — Customer Success Attrition & NRR Deep Dive
sourceDocuments.body:
**Prepared by:** VP Customer Success | **Date:** Oct 9

**NRR trend:** 112% → 108% → 106% (last three quarters)
**Gross retention:** 91% (down from 93.5%)

**Drivers of decline:**
1. Reconcile.io cohort churning at 14% annualized — integration friction; customers expected unified UX in Q2, slipped to Q1'26
2. Mid-market segment ($25-100K ACV) seeing budget compression; 6 logos downsold in Q3 alone
3. CSM ratio degraded from 1:18 to 1:26 accounts after we held CS hiring flat in H1

**Team health:** CSM voluntary attrition 22% TTM (benchmark: 14%). Exit interviews cite book-size, comp plan changes, and unclear career path. Two senior CSMs left for competitor in September.

**Ask:** 8 CSM hires + 2 CS Ops in FY26 ($1.4M fully loaded) to restore ratio and stabilize NRR. Without this, modeling NRR drifts to 102-104%.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 3 — Reconcile.io Integration Status Report
sourceDocuments.body:
**Prepared by:** Integration PMO Lead | **Date:** Oct 17

**Original thesis:** $14M purchase price; expected $4M cost synergies by month 12, unified product by month 9.

**Status at month 8:**
- Cost synergies realized: $1.6M of $4M (G&A consolidation done; infra consolidation behind — Reconcile still on separate AWS org)
- Product unification: 40% complete; data model migration is harder than scoped. Eng estimates 2 additional quarters
- Reconcile FTE retention: 71% (lost 2 of 3 senior engineers)
- Cross-sell: 19 deals closed combining both products ($2.1M ARR), below the 35-deal plan

**Risks:** If we pull eng resources to chase 2026 growth initiatives, integration slips to month 18+, and the Reconcile customer cohort (responsible for 40% of GRR decline) keeps churning. PMO recommends finishing integration before launching net-new platform bets.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 4 — Engineering Velocity & Capacity Memo
sourceDocuments.body:
**Prepared by:** VP Engineering | **Date:** Oct 11

**Capacity (FY26 planned):** 142 engineers; ~118 productive after on-call, support, security work.

**Allocation pressure:**
- Reconcile integration: 22 eng (PMO says needs 28 to hit revised timeline)
- Platform reliability/SOC2 Type II renewal: 14 eng (non-negotiable)
- Net-new AI features (CEO priority for reaccel narrative): 18 eng requested, 10 currently allocated
- Roadmap commitments to top 20 accounts: 24 eng
- Remaining: ~30 eng for everything else (mid-market roadmap, partner integrations, tech debt)

**Velocity:** Story points per eng down 8% YoY. Root causes: tech debt in core billing module; 2 reorgs in 12 months; senior IC attrition (12% TTM, mostly to AI-native startups offering refresh equity).

**Bottom line:** We cannot fund both a credible AI bet AND finish Reconcile integration on current headcount. Need +18 eng or a deprioritization decision.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 5 — Competitive & Market Intel Brief
sourceDocuments.body:
**Prepared by:** Head of Product Marketing | **Date:** Oct 6

**Competitive landscape shift:**
- Incumbent #1 (public, $400M ARR) launched AI-native reconciliation Sept; pricing 30% below ours on Reconcile.io overlap
- Two AI-native startups (Series B, $80M+ raised each) targeting our ICP with agent-based workflows; win rates against us up from 12% → 24% in competitive deals over 6 months
- Win rate vs. legacy incumbents stable at 58%

**Analyst view (Forrester, Gartner briefings):** Both analysts flagged Lattica as 'at risk of being leapfrogged on AI' in next wave reports. Customer references in advisory calls cite our roadmap as 'evolutionary, not revolutionary.'

**Pricing pressure:** ASP on new logo deals down 6% QoQ. 4 of last 10 losses cited 'AI capability gap' as primary reason.

**Recommendation:** Need a credible AI platform story by Q2'26 or risk structural win-rate decline in 2H'26.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 6 — Board Pre-Read Notes from Lead Director
sourceDocuments.body:
**From:** Lead Independent Director | **Date:** Oct 19 | **Channel:** email to CEO/COO/CFO

Team — sharing context ahead of the Nov offsite so we're aligned on what the board will press on.

1. **Growth narrative is the #1 issue.** Public comps decelerating from 20%+ to teens are trading at 4-5x ARR vs 8-10x for those reaccelerating. Two of our peers got taken private at discounts this year. We cannot present a 14% growth plan and expect support.

2. **But — no heroics on margin.** Two directors remain scarred from the 2022 burn era. Plan must hold op margin ≥15%. Going back below profitability is off the table.

3. **Integration credibility.** Reconcile.io was my deal sponsorship. The board will scrutinize whether we're delivering. Don't paper over it.

4. **AI posture.** Expect hard questions on whether we're a buyer, builder, or partner on AI. Have a point of view.

5. Three scenarios with explicit tradeoffs, please. Not a single recommendation dressed up as options.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 7 — Sales Pipeline & GTM Capacity Review
sourceDocuments.body:
**Prepared by:** VP Sales & RevOps Lead | **Date:** Oct 15

**Pipeline health:**
- Q4 pipeline coverage: 3.2x (healthy)
- FY26 pipeline build: 1.8x at this point in cycle (target 2.4x); enterprise segment soft
- New logo ACV: $58K avg, down from $64K; cycle length 94 days, up from 81

**Capacity:**
- 42 quota-carrying AEs; 71% to quota TTM (down from 78%)
- Ramp: new AE productivity ramp now 7.5 months (was 5.5) — VP Sales attributes to product complexity post-Reconcile and weaker enablement
- Marketing-sourced pipeline: 38% (target 50%); demand gen efficiency declined as we cut paid spend in H1 to protect margin

**Asks for FY26 reaccel case:**
- +12 AEs ($3.2M loaded), +6 SDRs ($720K), +$4M paid demand gen
- Total GTM investment delta: ~$8M, or 250bps of margin
- Forecasted impact: 19-22% growth vs 14% baseline, IF AI product narrative lands by Q2

question: Synthesize a scenario plan memo for the November board offsite presenting three FY26 operating cases (defend-margin, balanced, reaccelerate-growth). For each, specify the operational tradeoffs across integration, engineering capacity, CS staffing, and GTM investment, and recommend which case I should advocate for — with the operational risks I'm willing to own and those I'm not.

personaText:
Persona: VP Finance at Lattica, a Series B B2B SaaS company providing AI-powered contract lifecycle management to mid-market legal teams. Reports to the CFO; owns FP&A, financial reporting, and budget management. Lattica raised a $32M Series B nine months ago, is at $14M ARR, burning $1.3M/month with ~13 months of runway.

Scenario: The board has asked the CFO and VP Finance to bring three scenario plans to the next board meeting given mixed Q3 signals: pipeline softness, an aggressive new competitor, but a major upsell motion gaining traction. The CEO wants the team aligned on which scenario to operationalize before December planning kicks off, and the VP Finance must synthesize cross-functional inputs into a recommended path.
scenario: The board has asked the CFO and VP Finance to bring three scenario plans to the next board meeting given mixed Q3 signals: pipeline softness, an aggressive new competitor, but a major upsell motion gaining traction. The CEO wants the team aligned on which scenario to operationalize before December planning kicks off, and the VP Finance must synthesize cross-functional inputs into a recommended path.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 1 — Q3 P&L and Runway Snapshot
sourceDocuments.body:
**Q3 2024 Financial Summary (prepared by FP&A)**

- ARR: $14.2M (vs. $13.8M plan; +3% beat driven by expansion)
- Net New ARR Q3: $1.1M (plan: $1.6M; -31% miss on new logo)
- Gross Margin: 74% (stable)
- Q3 Operating Loss: ($4.1M)
- Cash on hand (Sept 30): $17.2M
- Monthly burn (trailing 3mo avg): $1.32M
- Implied runway at current burn: 13.0 months

**Headcount:** 142 FTE (up from 118 at Series B close)
- S&M: 48 (34%)
- R&D: 56 (39%)
- G&A: 22
- CS: 16

**Commentary:** Expansion ARR is carrying the quarter. New logo acquisition is materially behind plan for the second consecutive quarter. CAC payback has stretched from 18 to 24 months. Without intervention, runway falls below 12 months by end of Q1 2025. The CFO has flagged that any Series C conversation in H2 2025 will require either >$20M ARR with healthy efficiency metrics or a clear path to default-alive within 18 months.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 2 — Pipeline Review Memo from VP Sales
sourceDocuments.body:
**To:** CFO, VP Finance
**From:** VP Sales
**Re:** Q4 Pipeline & 2025 Outlook

Q4 pipeline coverage stands at 2.8x (target 3.5x). Top-of-funnel MQLs down 22% QoQ. Three observations:

1. **Mid-market deals slowing.** Average sales cycle has extended from 71 to 94 days. Procurement scrutiny on net-new SaaS spend is the #1 cited reason in lost-deal analysis.

2. **Competitive losses up.** We lost 7 of 19 competitive deals to Ironclad-backed entrant 'Clausemind' in Q3 (vs. 2 of 14 in Q2). They are pricing 30-40% below us and bundling AI redlining free.

3. **Expansion is the bright spot.** Existing-customer upsell pipeline is $4.2M for Q4, with 65% weighted. The new 'Clause Intelligence' module is driving 90% of expansion conversations.

**Ask:** I need 4 more AEs to hit 2025 plan ($24M ARR). Alternatively, if we refocus 6 AEs onto expansion-only, I believe we can hit $21M ARR with the current team but new logo growth stalls.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 3 — Competitor Intelligence Brief: Clausemind
sourceDocuments.body:
**Source:** Product marketing + 4 win/loss interviews, Sept 2024

Clausemind launched 14 months ago, raised $18M Series A in July. Aggressively targeting our ICP (legal teams at 200-2000 employee companies).

**Pricing:** $32/seat/mo vs. our $58/seat/mo. Free AI redlining tier (we charge $15K/yr add-on).

**Product gaps (vs. Lattica):** Weaker integrations (no Salesforce CPQ), no audit trail certification, limited reporting. Their NPS reportedly 31 vs. our 52.

**Sales motion:** PLG + inside sales. Land at $20-40K, expand. They closed ~80 logos in 9 months.

**Buyer feedback (from lost deals):** 'Lattica is the better product but we couldn't justify 2x the cost for features we won't use in year one.' — GC at 600-person fintech

**Strategic read:** They are unlikely to win enterprise (>2000 employees) but are eating our SMB and lower mid-market. If we don't respond on packaging within 2 quarters, our new-logo ACV will continue to compress.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 4 — Customer Success / Churn Analysis
sourceDocuments.body:
**Q3 Retention Metrics (CS Ops)**

- Gross Revenue Retention: 91% (Q2: 93%)
- Net Revenue Retention: 118% (Q2: 114%) — driven by Clause Intelligence upsell
- Logo churn: 4 customers, $340K ARR (3 SMB, 1 mid-market)
- At-risk ARR (red/yellow health): $2.1M across 14 accounts

**Churn drivers (exit interviews):**
- 2 churned to Clausemind explicitly on price
- 1 acquired and consolidated onto incumbent's tool
- 1 SMB cited 'too complex for our team size'

**Expansion signal:** 22 customers have activated Clause Intelligence in pilot. Conversion-to-paid running at 73%. Average expansion uplift: $28K ARR per account.

**CS Lead recommendation:** Hire 2 more CSMs focused on expansion playbook execution. Current CSM:ARR ratio is 1:$890K, industry benchmark 1:$1.2M but we are leaving expansion on the table. Estimated incremental ARR: $2-3M over next 12 months from 2 hires.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 5 — Engineering Velocity & Roadmap Status
sourceDocuments.body:
**Engineering Update — VP Engineering**

**Velocity:** 38 story points/sprint avg (down from 44 in Q2; attributed to onboarding 8 new hires + 2 senior departures).

**Roadmap status:**
- Clause Intelligence v2 (expansion driver): on track, GA Nov 15
- Salesforce CPQ deep integration (enterprise unlock): 6 weeks behind, now Q1
- SMB self-serve onboarding (PLG response to Clausemind): NOT YET STAFFED. Estimated 2 squads x 4 months = ~$800K loaded cost
- SOC 2 Type II + FedRAMP Ready: external auditor engaged, $400K project

**Tradeoff flagged:** We cannot ship self-serve PLG, Salesforce CPQ, AND maintain Clause Intelligence v3 roadmap with current team. Need to pick two, or add 6 engineers (~$1.4M annualized).

**Talent risk:** Two senior ICs have received outside offers. Retention bonuses requested ($180K total).

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 6 — Board Meeting Pre-Read Notes (Sept Board)
sourceDocuments.body:
**Excerpts from Sept 12 board discussion (recorded by Chief of Staff)**

**Lead investor (Series B partner):** 'We underwrote Lattica on enterprise mid-market dominance. The Clausemind threat is real but I do NOT want to see us race to the bottom on price. Show me a path to $25M ARR with NRR >120% and we'll lead the C.'

**Independent director (operator):** 'Burn is the issue. You have 13 months. If new logo isn't working, stop forcing it. Double down on expansion, cut S&M by 20%, extend runway to 18+ months. Series C in a tougher market needs more cushion.'

**Seed investor:** 'Don't abandon new logo. The TAM compounding requires logo growth. Find a packaging answer to Clausemind — a lower SKU — within one quarter.'

**CEO summary ask:** Bring three scenarios to November board: (A) Growth-at-plan (raise bridge if needed), (B) Efficient growth (rebalance to expansion, modest S&M cut), (C) Default-alive (aggressive cuts, 20+ months runway). Recommend one.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 7 — 2025 Hiring Plan & Compensation Forecast
sourceDocuments.body:
**2025 Hiring Plan Draft (People Ops + Finance)**

**Plan-of-record (built at Series B close):** +52 hires in 2025, ending headcount 194.
- S&M: +18 (4 AEs, 6 SDRs, 4 CSMs, 2 marketing, 2 RevOps)
- R&D: +24
- G&A: +6
- CS: +4

**Fully-loaded annual cost of plan-of-record:** $11.2M incremental.

**Scenario re-runs (requested by CFO):**
- **Scenario A (Growth):** +52 hires as planned. 2025 burn: $19M. Runway exit-2025: ~5 months. Requires bridge or accelerated C.
- **Scenario B (Rebalance):** +28 hires (skew to R&D + CS, freeze new-logo S&M). 2025 burn: $13M. Runway exit-2025: ~10 months.
- **Scenario C (Default-alive):** +12 hires (critical R&D only), 8-10 RIFs in S&M. 2025 burn: $8M. Runway exit-2025: ~17 months.

**Retention overhang:** $1.1M in unplanned retention/refresh equity grants flagged by CPO across all scenarios. Comp bands trailing market by ~8% in eng.

question: Synthesize a board-ready scenario recommendation memo: evaluate the three scenarios (Growth-at-plan, Efficient Rebalance, Default-alive) against the cross-functional signals — pipeline softness, Clausemind competitive pressure, expansion momentum, engineering capacity tradeoffs, and divergent board-member preferences — and recommend one scenario with the operating model changes, financial targets, and key risks the CFO and I should commit to in November.

1. **Mid-market deals slowing.** Average sales cycle has extended from 71 to 94 days. Procurement scrutiny on net-new SaaS spend is the #1 cited reason in lost-deal analysis.

2. **Competitive losses up.** We lost 7 of 19 competitive deals to Ironclad-backed entrant 'Clausemind' in Q3 (vs. 2 of 14 in Q2). They are pricing 30-40% below us and bundling AI redlining free.

3. **Expansion is the bright spot.** Existing-customer upsell pipeline is $4.2M for Q4, with 65% weighted. The new 'Clause Intelligence' module is driving 90% of expansion conversations.

**Ask:** I need 4 more AEs to hit 2025 plan ($24M ARR). Alternatively, if we refocus 6 AEs onto expansion-only, I believe we can hit $21M ARR with the current team but new logo growth stalls.

---

## DOC 3 — Competitor Intelligence Brief: Clausemind

**Source:** Product marketing + 4 win/loss interviews, Sept 2024

Clausemind launched 14 months ago, raised $18M Series A in July. Aggressively targeting our ICP (legal teams at 200-2000 employee companies).

**Pricing:** $32/seat/mo vs. our $58/seat/mo. Free AI redlining tier (we charge $15K/yr add-on).

**Product gaps (vs. Lattica):** Weaker integrations (no Salesforce CPQ), no audit trail certification, limited reporting. Their NPS reportedly 31 vs. our 52.

**Sales motion:** PLG + inside sales. Land at $20-40K, expand. They closed ~80 logos in 9 months.

**Buyer feedback (from lost deals):** 'Lattica is the better product but we couldn't justify 2x the cost for features we won't use in year one.' — GC at 600-person fintech

**Strategic read:** They are unlikely to win enterprise (>2000 employees) but are eating our SMB and lower mid-market. If we don't respond on packaging within 2 quarters, our new-logo ACV will continue to compress.

---

## DOC 4 — Customer Success / Churn Analysis

**Q3 Retention Metrics (CS Ops)**

- Gross Revenue Retention: 91% (Q2: 93%)
- Net Revenue Retention: 118% (Q2: 114%) — driven by Clause Intelligence upsell
- Logo churn: 4 customers, $340K ARR (3 SMB, 1 mid-market)
- At-risk ARR (red/yellow health): $2.1M across 14 accounts

**Churn drivers (exit interviews):**
- 2 churned to Clausemind explicitly on price
- 1 acquired and consolidated onto incumbent's tool
- 1 SMB cited 'too complex for our team size'

**Expansion signal:** 22 customers have activated Clause Intelligence in pilot. Conversion-to-paid running at 73%. Average expansion uplift: $28K ARR per account.

**CS Lead recommendation:** Hire 2 more CSMs focused on expansion playbook execution. Current CSM:ARR ratio is 1:$890K, industry benchmark 1:$1.2M but we are leaving expansion on the table. Estimated incremental ARR: $2-3M over next 12 months from 2 hires.

---

## DOC 5 — Engineering Velocity & Roadmap Status

**Engineering Update — VP Engineering**

**Velocity:** 38 story points/sprint avg (down from 44 in Q2; attributed to onboarding 8 new hires + 2 senior departures).

**Roadmap status:**
- Clause Intelligence v2 (expansion driver): on track, GA Nov 15
- Salesforce CPQ deep integration (enterprise unlock): 6 weeks behind, now Q1
- SMB self-serve onboarding (PLG response to Clausemind): NOT YET STAFFED. Estimated 2 squads x 4 months = ~$800K loaded cost
- SOC 2 Type II + FedRAMP Ready: external auditor engaged, $400K project

**Tradeoff flagged:** We cannot ship self-serve PLG, Salesforce CPQ, AND maintain Clause Intelligence v3 roadmap with current team. Need to pick two, or add 6 engineers (~$1.4M annualized).

**Talent risk:** Two senior ICs have received outside offers. Retention bonuses requested ($180K total).

---

## DOC 6 — Board Meeting Pre-Read Notes (Sept Board)

**Excerpts from Sept 12 board discussion (recorded by Chief of Staff)**

**Lead investor (Series B partner):** 'We underwrote Lattica on enterprise mid-market dominance. The Clausemind threat is real but I do NOT want to see us race to the bottom on price. Show me a path to $25M ARR with NRR >120% and we'll lead the C.'

**Independent director (operator):** 'Burn is the issue. You have 13 months. If new logo isn't working, stop forcing it. Double down on expansion, cut S&M by 20%, extend runway to 18+ months. Series C in a tougher market needs more cushion.'

**Seed investor:** 'Don't abandon new logo. The TAM compounding requires logo growth. Find a packaging answer to Clausemind — a lower SKU — within one quarter.'

**CEO summary ask:** Bring three scenarios to November board: (A) Growth-at-plan (raise bridge if needed), (B) Efficient growth (rebalance to expansion, modest S&M cut), (C) Default-alive (aggressive cuts, 20+ months runway). Recommend one.

---

## DOC 7 — 2025 Hiring Plan & Compensation Forecast

**2025 Hiring Plan Draft (People Ops + Finance)**

**Plan-of-record (built at Series B close):** +52 hires in 2025, ending headcount 194.
- S&M: +18 (4 AEs, 6 SDRs, 4 CSMs, 2 marketing, 2 RevOps)
- R&D: +24
- G&A: +6
- CS: +4

**Fully-loaded annual cost of plan-of-record:** $11.2M incremental.

**Scenario re-runs (requested by CFO):**
- **Scenario A (Growth):** +52 hires as planned. 2025 burn: $19M. Runway exit-2025: ~5 months. Requires bridge or accelerated C.
- **Scenario B (Rebalance):** +28 hires (skew to R&D + CS, freeze new-logo S&M). 2025 burn: $13M. Runway exit-2025: ~10 months.
- **Scenario C (Default-alive):** +12 hires (critical R&D only), 8-10 RIFs in S&M. 2025 burn: $8M. Runway exit-2025: ~17 months.

**Retention overhang:** $1.1M in unplanned retention/refresh equity grants flagged by CPO across all scenarios. Comp bands trailing market by ~8% in eng.

personaText:
Persona: VP Finance at Lattixe, a $62M ARR B2B SaaS vendor selling workflow automation to mid-market insurance and financial services firms. Reached GAAP profitability six quarters ago, currently running 19% operating margin with $87M cash on hand. Reports to the CFO and owns FP&A, the annual planning cycle, and quarterly board financials.

Scenario: The CFO has asked me to lead a scenario planning exercise for FY26 ahead of next month's board strategy offsite. Growth has decelerated from 38% to 22% YoY, two recent acquisitions are still being integrated, and the board's audit committee chair has flagged concerns that we are over-indexing on margin protection at the expense of reinvestment. I need to model three coherent scenarios and recommend one for the board to anchor on.
scenario: The CFO has asked me to lead a scenario planning exercise for FY26 ahead of next month's board strategy offsite. Growth has decelerated from 38% to 22% YoY, two recent acquisitions are still being integrated, and the board's audit committee chair has flagged concerns that we are over-indexing on margin protection at the expense of reinvestment. I need to model three coherent scenarios and recommend one for the board to anchor on.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 1 — FY25 Q3 P&L Summary and Trailing Metrics
sourceDocuments.body:
## Lattixe Q3 FY25 Financial Summary

**Revenue & Margin**
- Q3 ARR: $62.1M (+22% YoY, vs. +38% YoY in Q3 FY24)
- Q3 Revenue: $15.4M; GAAP Operating Margin: 19.2%
- FCF Margin (TTM): 21.4%
- Gross Margin: 78.1% (down 110 bps YoY due to Helios acquisition hosting costs)

**Spend Composition (Q3)**
- S&M: 32% of revenue (down from 41% two years ago)
- R&D: 22% of revenue (down from 27%)
- G&A: 13% of revenue (elevated due to integration costs)

**Efficiency**
- Net Revenue Retention: 108% (down from 119% in FY23)
- Gross Retention: 91%
- Magic Number: 0.7 (below 1.0 for three consecutive quarters)
- Rule of 40: 41 (composition shifting from growth to margin)

**Commentary from Controller:** Margin expansion has come primarily from S&M discipline and headcount freezes in non-engineering functions. Pipeline coverage for Q4 is 2.8x (target 3.5x). The Helios and Brightpath integrations are consuming roughly $4.2M of annualized G&A that should normalize by Q2 FY26.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 2 — Board Strategy Pre-Read: Audit Committee Chair Memo
sourceDocuments.body:
## Memo from Audit Committee Chair to Full Board
*Re: Concerns ahead of FY26 planning offsite*

Colleagues — I want to flag a pattern I've seen at three prior portfolio companies that I believe Lattixe is now exhibiting.

**The complacency signal.** Management has done excellent work hitting Rule of 40 and crossing into durable profitability. However, the trajectory of growth deceleration (38 → 30 → 26 → 22%) combined with Magic Number below 1.0 suggests we are under-investing in the growth engine, not simply harvesting efficiency.

**Specific concerns:**
1. S&M as % of revenue has compressed faster than peer median (Meritech SaaS index: 38%; Lattixe: 32%).
2. R&D headcount has been flat for 5 quarters while we acquired two products that need platform consolidation.
3. The CEO's narrative to Wall Street analysts emphasizes margin, but our public comps (n=12) trading at premium multiples are growth-led, not margin-led.

**Ask of management at offsite:** Present at least one scenario that contemplates *reinvestment* — accepting margin compression to 10-12% in exchange for re-acceleration. I do not want a single-scenario plan dressed up as three options.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 3 — Sales Pipeline & Capacity Review (VP Sales)
sourceDocuments.body:
## Q4 FY25 Pipeline Council Notes
*Prepared by VP Sales for ELT*

**Pipeline State**
- Q4 weighted pipeline: $11.8M new ARR (coverage 2.8x vs. 3.5x target)
- FY26 early pipeline: $34M, but only 22% sourced from outbound (target 40%)
- Win rates stable at 28% on qualified opps; ASP up 14% YoY ($87K)

**Capacity Constraints**
- 47 quota-carrying AEs; attainment 61% (vs. 72% in FY24)
- 9 open AE reqs frozen since Q1 under margin protection mandate
- SDR team cut 30% in FY24 reorganization — pipeline generation now bottleneck, not conversion

**VP Sales position:** "We are leaving roughly $8-12M of FY26 ARR on the table by not refilling SDR capacity and the 9 frozen AE seats. Payback on a fully-ramped AE is 14 months at current ASP and win rates. If we hire in Q1 FY26, those reps contribute meaningfully in H2."

**Risk flag:** Two enterprise logos ($2.1M combined ARR) signaled they are evaluating Workato and Tines for adjacent use cases we could win with deeper integrations team.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 4 — Helios & Brightpath Integration Status (Chief of Staff)
sourceDocuments.body:
## M&A Integration Tracker — End of Q3 FY25

**Helios (acquired Q1 FY24, $34M)**
- Revenue synergy realization: 41% of Year-2 plan ($3.2M of $7.8M target)
- Platform consolidation: 60% complete; remaining work estimated at 14 engineering FTE-quarters
- Customer churn on Helios book: 14% gross (vs. 9% pre-acquisition) — attributed to delayed roadmap clarity

**Brightpath (acquired Q3 FY24, $19M)**
- Revenue synergy: tracking to plan ($1.1M of $1.4M Year-1)
- Cultural integration: 4 of 6 senior Brightpath engineers departed
- Cross-sell motion not yet launched (originally planned Q3 FY25)

**Chief of Staff commentary:** "We are being asked to deliver integration synergies AND ship the consolidated platform AND not increase R&D headcount. Two of three is achievable; all three is not. The CTO has been clear that without 8-12 additional engineers, platform consolidation slips to FY27, which jeopardizes the Helios revenue synergy thesis the board approved."

**Cash impact:** Total deal earn-outs of $11M tied to FY26 revenue milestones currently at risk.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 5 — Wall Street Analyst & Investor Sentiment Digest
sourceDocuments.body:
## IR Digest: Sell-Side & Buy-Side Commentary, October FY25
*Compiled by Head of Investor Relations*

**Sell-side notes (5 covering analysts):**
- Goldman: "Lattixe has demonstrated rare profitable-growth balance, but deceleration to low-20s% raises questions about TAM penetration. Reiterate Buy, lower PT from $54 to $48."
- Morgan Stanley: "Margin story is intact; growth story is not. Concerned that the Helios integration is masking organic deceleration."
- Two analysts downgraded from Buy to Hold citing "insufficient visibility into FY26 reacceleration catalysts."

**Buy-side feedback from recent NDR (12 funds):**
- 7 of 12 funds explicitly asked: "Why aren't you reinvesting given 21% FCF margin and $87M cash?"
- 3 funds said they would prefer 12-15% margin with 28%+ growth
- Only 2 funds preferred current margin profile

**Stock performance:** -18% over trailing 6 months vs. -4% for SaaS index.

**IR recommendation:** "We need a credible reinvestment narrative for the Q4 earnings call or we will see further multiple compression. The market is telling us it has paid us for margin and now wants growth."

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 6 — Competitive Intelligence Brief (Strategy Team)
sourceDocuments.body:
## Competitive Landscape Brief — FY26 Planning Input

**Direct competitor moves (last 2 quarters):**
- **Workato:** Raised $200M Series F at $5.7B; announced 40% R&D headcount expansion, targeting insurance vertical (our core).
- **Tines:** Launched financial services compliance module — direct overlap with Helios product line. Pricing 15% below Lattixe equivalent.
- **Pega (incumbent):** Aggressive renegotiation tactics on renewals; we have lost 4 of 11 competitive renewals in FY25 vs. 1 of 9 in FY24.

**Adjacent threats:**
- Two well-funded private companies (Tray.ai, n8n enterprise) entering mid-market with platform plays
- Microsoft Power Automate continues to capture low-end of our SMB segment

**Win/loss themes from last 30 deals:**
- Won deals: integration depth, vertical compliance features, customer references
- Lost deals (pattern): "Lattixe roadmap unclear post-acquisitions," "competitor shipping faster," pricing only #3 reason

**Strategy team conclusion:** "The window to consolidate vertical leadership in insurance/financial services is 18-24 months. Workato's capital raise materially changes the competitive calculus. A defensive posture invites share loss."

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 7 — Three-Year Driver-Based Financial Model (FP&A Team)
sourceDocuments.body:
## Lattixe FY26-FY28 Driver Model — Working Draft
*Prepared by FP&A Director for VP Finance review*

**Scenario A — Margin Protection (current trajectory)**
- Headcount growth: +4% (replacement only)
- FY26 ARR: $73M (+18%); FY27: $84M (+15%); FY28: $94M (+12%)
- Operating margin: 21% → 23% → 24%
- Cumulative FCF FY26-28: $58M

**Scenario B — Balanced Reinvestment**
- Headcount: +14% (refill 9 AE seats, +12 engineers, rebuild SDR team)
- FY26 ARR: $77M (+24%); FY27: $96M (+25%); FY28: $118M (+23%)
- Operating margin: 14% → 16% → 19%
- Cumulative FCF FY26-28: $42M; trough cash: $71M (Q3 FY26)

**Scenario C — Aggressive Reacceleration**
- Headcount: +28%; international expansion (UK, DACH); 2nd product line investment
- FY26 ARR: $80M (+29%); FY27: $107M (+34%); FY28: $140M (+31%)
- Operating margin: 8% → 11% → 16%
- Cumulative FCF FY26-28: $19M; trough cash: $58M; would require board approval to dip below 18-month rule

**Sensitivities not yet modeled:** Helios churn worsening, Workato vertical entry impact on win rates, FX on international plan.

**FP&A Director note:** "Scenario B assumes Magic Number recovers to 0.9 within 4 quarters — historical precedent at our stage suggests 5-6 quarters is more realistic. We should stress-test that."

question: Synthesize across the docs and draft a board pre-read memo recommending one of the three FY26 scenarios (A/B/C). Justify the choice by reconciling the audit committee's reinvestment pressure, Wall Street's multiple-compression signal, integration capacity constraints, and competitive timing — and specify which assumptions in the FP&A model most need stress-testing before the offsite.

personaText:
Persona: You are an Independent Director on the board of Lattermark, a Series B B2B SaaS company providing AI-driven contract lifecycle management to mid-market legal and procurement teams. You sit on the Audit Committee and were recruited for governance depth and SaaS operating experience; the company recently closed a $32M Series B, has ~14 months of runway at current burn ($1.3M/month), and is at $14.2M ARR.

Scenario: The CEO has called a special board session in three weeks to align on a 2024 operating plan after Q3 missed net-new ARR by 22% and a key competitor (Ironclad) just announced a price-disruptive SMB tier. The lead investor is pushing for a 'capital efficiency reset,' the CEO is advocating doubling down on enterprise expansion, and the CFO has floated a contingency RIF. As an independent director, you've been asked to lead a scenario-planning exercise so the board can stress-test 2024 paths before approving the plan.
scenario: The CEO has called a special board session in three weeks to align on a 2024 operating plan after Q3 missed net-new ARR by 22% and a key competitor (Ironclad) just announced a price-disruptive SMB tier. The lead investor is pushing for a 'capital efficiency reset,' the CEO is advocating doubling down on enterprise expansion, and the CFO has floated a contingency RIF. As an independent director, you've been asked to lead a scenario-planning exercise so the board can stress-test 2024 paths before approving the plan.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 1 — Q3 2023 Board Financial Pack (excerpt)
sourceDocuments.body:
**Q3 2023 Financial Summary — prepared by CFO**

- ARR: $14.2M (vs plan $15.6M; -9%)
- Net new ARR Q3: $0.9M (vs plan $1.15M; -22%)
- Gross margin: 71% (down from 74% Q2 — driven by AWS overage + new CSM hires loaded to COGS)
- Cash: $28.4M; monthly burn $1.3M; implied runway ~14 months at flat burn, ~21 months if burn cut to $850K
- Magic Number (TTM): 0.6 (down from 0.9)
- Rule of 40: 28% (growth 41% YoY + FCF margin -13%)
- Net Revenue Retention: 108% (down from 116% — see churn deck)
- S&M as % of revenue: 62% (peer median ~48%)

**CFO commentary:** 'Efficiency metrics deteriorated meaningfully this quarter. We are spending Series B capital faster than the unit economics justify. Without intervention, we will need to raise an extension round in Q3 2024 from a position of weakness. I recommend the board formally consider a 12-15% cost reduction scenario alongside the growth case.'

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 2 — CEO 2024 Strategic Plan Memo
sourceDocuments.body:
**To:** Board of Directors
**From:** CEO
**Re:** 2024 Operating Plan — 'Enterprise Acceleration'

The Q3 miss is real but masks signal: our 6 largest deals (>$200K ACV) all closed on time and expansion in the enterprise segment grew 34% QoQ. The miss is concentrated in mid-market where Ironclad's new SMB tier is creating noise.

**Proposal:** Lean INTO enterprise. Specifically:
1. Hire 4 enterprise AEs + 2 solutions engineers in Q1 (incremental $2.4M annualized)
2. Build out a 'Legal AI' product pod (6 eng, 1 PM) — $1.8M
3. Hold mid-market headcount flat; do not chase Ironclad on price
4. Target 2024 ARR: $24M (+69%); burn increases to $1.6M/month; runway tightens to ~11 months but we raise Series C in Q4 2024 at $200M+ valuation off enterprise traction

I strongly oppose a RIF. We will lose key talent to competitors and signal weakness mid-cycle. The Series B thesis was growth; retreating now wastes the capital we just raised. I need the board's conviction here.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 3 — Lead Investor (Meridian Capital) Position Paper
sourceDocuments.body:
**From:** Partner, Meridian Capital (Series B lead, board observer)

We remain supportive of Lattermark but want to flag concerns ahead of the Nov board:

- Our portfolio data shows Series B companies with Magic Number <0.7 and burn multiple >2.0x face a 60% probability of a flat or down extension round. Lattermark's burn multiple is currently 1.7x trending toward 2.1x.
- Public SaaS comps trade at 6-7x ARR (down from 14x at your Series B). A Series C at $200M+ requires either $30M+ ARR with >50% growth OR exceptional efficiency (Rule of 40 >50%). Neither is the current trajectory.
- We would support: (a) a 2024 plan that achieves Rule of 40 >40% even at the cost of growth, (b) a scenario where burn is reduced to $900K/month, extending runway to 22+ months and removing financing risk.
- We would NOT support: aggressive hiring that further degrades the burn multiple absent clear leading indicators.

We respect the CEO's conviction but request the board formally evaluate at least three scenarios with sensitivities.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 4 — Churn & NRR Deep Dive (Customer Success)
sourceDocuments.body:
**Prepared by VP Customer Success — Q3 Retention Analysis**

- Logo churn TTM: 11% (up from 7% prior year)
- Gross dollar churn: 9%; Net Revenue Retention: 108%
- Churn concentration: 73% of churned ARR came from <$50K ACV mid-market customers; enterprise (>$150K ACV) gross retention is 96%
- Top cited reasons in exit interviews: (1) 'Ironclad SMB tier was 40% cheaper for features we actually use' — 8 of 14 exits, (2) implementation took >90 days — 4 of 14, (3) AI features 'underwhelming vs demos' — 5 of 14
- Expansion: enterprise NRR 128%; mid-market NRR 94%

**Commentary:** 'Mid-market is structurally challenged for us — CAC payback is 28 months vs 14 months in enterprise. I am skeptical we can fix mid-market retention without a dedicated lower-tier SKU, which Product has deprioritized. If we are not going to invest in mid-market, we should be honest that this segment will continue to bleed.'

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 5 — Competitive Intelligence Brief: Ironclad SMB Launch
sourceDocuments.body:
**Prepared by Head of Product Marketing — October 2023**

Ironclad announced 'Ironclad Starter' Oct 3: $12K/year entry price (vs our mid-market entry at $28K), targeting <500 employee companies. Includes: AI redlining, 5 templates, Salesforce integration. Notably EXCLUDES: custom workflows, advanced approvals, API access.

**Win/loss data Q3 (62 mid-market opps):**
- Lost to Ironclad: 18 (29%) — up from 11% in Q2
- Lost to DocuSign CLM: 7
- Lost to 'no decision': 14
- Won: 23 (37%)

**Field feedback:** 'In deals under $40K ACV we are getting compressed on price within 2 calls. AEs are discounting 25-35% to compete and still losing. In enterprise (>$150K) Ironclad rarely shows up — we win on workflow depth and security review.'

**Recommendation from PMM:** Either launch a competitive SMB tier within 2 quarters OR formally exit sub-$40K ACV segment to protect margin and focus.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 6 — Engineering Velocity & Talent Retention Report
sourceDocuments.body:
**From:** VP Engineering — Confidential to Board

**Velocity:**
- Story points shipped Q3: 412 (vs 478 Q2; -14%)
- 'Legal AI' roadmap items: 4 of 9 committed features shipped; 2 delayed to Q1, 3 descoped
- P0/P1 incidents: 7 in Q3 (vs 3 in Q2) — primarily AI inference reliability

**Talent:**
- Voluntary attrition TTM: 18% (industry benchmark ~13%)
- Lost in Q3: 2 senior ML engineers (to OpenAI, Anthropic), 1 staff backend (to Ironclad — flagged)
- Compensation review: 60th percentile vs market; recruiters report we are losing finalists on equity refresh
- Open roles: 14 (8 eng, 4 GTM, 2 G&A); average time-to-fill 71 days

**Commentary:** 'A RIF would be catastrophic for AI talent retention — these engineers have options weekly. However, the current org is overextended; we are committing to enterprise features, AI investment, and platform reliability with insufficient senior bandwidth. Pick two.'

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 7 — Audit Committee Risk Register (October 2023)
sourceDocuments.body:
**Top Enterprise Risks — reviewed by Audit Committee**

| # | Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Trend |
|---|------|-----------|--------|-------|
| 1 | Financing risk: unable to raise Series C on acceptable terms | Med-High | Severe | ↑ |
| 2 | Competitive displacement in mid-market (Ironclad) | High | High | ↑ |
| 3 | Key person / AI talent attrition | Med | High | ↑ |
| 4 | AI product reliability incidents → enterprise churn | Med | High | → |
| 5 | Customer concentration: top 10 = 38% of ARR | Med | Med | → |
| 6 | Cyber/SOC2 — pending Type II audit Q1 | Low | High | → |
| 7 | Founder-CEO key person risk; no COO | Med | Med | ↑ |

**Independent Chair note:** 'The interaction between Risk #1 and Risks #2/#3 is what concerns the committee most. Aggressive cost cuts mitigate #1 but inflame #3. Aggressive growth mitigates #2/#3 but worsens #1. The board needs an explicit framework for navigating this tradeoff, not a binary choice between the CEO and CFO positions. Recommend dedicated scenario session before plan approval.'

question: As the independent director leading the scenario-planning session, draft a board memo that defines 3 distinct 2024 operating scenarios (growth-leaning, efficiency-leaning, and a balanced 'third path'), specifies the key financial/operational triggers and tradeoffs for each, identifies which board-level risks each scenario amplifies or mitigates, and recommends a decision framework — including pre-committed tripwires — for choosing among them at the November board meeting.

## DOC 1 — Q3 2023 Board Financial Pack (excerpt)

**Q3 2023 Financial Summary — prepared by CFO**

- ARR: $14.2M (vs plan $15.6M; -9%)
- Net new ARR Q3: $0.9M (vs plan $1.15M; -22%)
- Gross margin: 71% (down from 74% Q2 — driven by AWS overage + new CSM hires loaded to COGS)
- Cash: $28.4M; monthly burn $1.3M; implied runway ~14 months at flat burn, ~21 months if burn cut to $850K
- Magic Number (TTM): 0.6 (down from 0.9)
- Rule of 40: 28% (growth 41% YoY + FCF margin -13%)
- Net Revenue Retention: 108% (down from 116% — see churn deck)
- S&M as % of revenue: 62% (peer median ~48%)

**CFO commentary:** 'Efficiency metrics deteriorated meaningfully this quarter. We are spending Series B capital faster than the unit economics justify. Without intervention, we will need to raise an extension round in Q3 2024 from a position of weakness. I recommend the board formally consider a 12-15% cost reduction scenario alongside the growth case.'

---

## DOC 2 — CEO 2024 Strategic Plan Memo

**To:** Board of Directors
**From:** CEO
**Re:** 2024 Operating Plan — 'Enterprise Acceleration'

The Q3 miss is real but masks signal: our 6 largest deals (>$200K ACV) all closed on time and expansion in the enterprise segment grew 34% QoQ. The miss is concentrated in mid-market where Ironclad's new SMB tier is creating noise.

**Proposal:** Lean INTO enterprise. Specifically:
1. Hire 4 enterprise AEs + 2 solutions engineers in Q1 (incremental $2.4M annualized)
2. Build out a 'Legal AI' product pod (6 eng, 1 PM) — $1.8M
3. Hold mid-market headcount flat; do not chase Ironclad on price
4. Target 2024 ARR: $24M (+69%); burn increases to $1.6M/month; runway tightens to ~11 months but we raise Series C in Q4 2024 at $200M+ valuation off enterprise traction

I strongly oppose a RIF. We will lose key talent to competitors and signal weakness mid-cycle. The Series B thesis was growth; retreating now wastes the capital we just raised. I need the board's conviction here.

---

## DOC 3 — Lead Investor (Meridian Capital) Position Paper

**From:** Partner, Meridian Capital (Series B lead, board observer)

We remain supportive of Lattermark but want to flag concerns ahead of the Nov board:

- Our portfolio data shows Series B companies with Magic Number <0.7 and burn multiple >2.0x face a 60% probability of a flat or down extension round. Lattermark's burn multiple is currently 1.7x trending toward 2.1x.
- Public SaaS comps trade at 6-7x ARR (down from 14x at your Series B). A Series C at $200M+ requires either $30M+ ARR with >50% growth OR exceptional efficiency (Rule of 40 >50%). Neither is the current trajectory.
- We would support: (a) a 2024 plan that achieves Rule of 40 >40% even at the cost of growth, (b) a scenario where burn is reduced to $900K/month, extending runway to 22+ months and removing financing risk.
- We would NOT support: aggressive hiring that further degrades the burn multiple absent clear leading indicators.

We respect the CEO's conviction but request the board formally evaluate at least three scenarios with sensitivities.

---

I am skeptical we can fix mid-market retention without a dedicated lower-tier SKU, which Product has deprioritized. personaText:
Persona: You are an Independent Director on the board of Meridian Cloud Analytics, a B2B SaaS company that crossed into GAAP profitability six quarters ago and now runs at $62M ARR with a 19% operating margin. You chair the Risk & Strategy committee and the lead director has asked you to drive scenario planning ahead of the Q4 board offsite.

Scenario: Growth has decelerated from 41% YoY two years ago to 22% this quarter, a recently acquired workflow company is mid-integration, and a well-funded entrant has begun undercutting on price in the mid-market. The CEO is proposing to defend margin and lean into a large platform bet; two other directors are pushing for a more aggressive growth reinvestment. You need to frame three coherent forward scenarios for the board, with triggers and governance guardrails, before management's strategy memo is finalized.
scenario: Growth has decelerated from 41% YoY two years ago to 22% this quarter, a recently acquired workflow company is mid-integration, and a well-funded entrant has begun undercutting on price in the mid-market. The CEO is proposing to defend margin and lean into a large platform bet; two other directors are pushing for a more aggressive growth reinvestment. You need to frame three coherent forward scenarios for the board, with triggers and governance guardrails, before management's strategy memo is finalized.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 1 — Q3 FY25 P&L and KPI Summary (CFO Pack)
sourceDocuments.body:
**Meridian Cloud Analytics — Q3 FY25**

- ARR: $62.4M (+22% YoY, vs. +28% guided at start of year)
- Net New ARR: $4.1M (Q3'24: $5.6M)
- GAAP Operating Margin: 19.2% (Q2: 18.1%)
- FCF Margin: 23%; Cash on hand: $148M; no debt
- Gross Margin: 78% (steady)
- NRR: 108% (down from 117% a year ago)
- GRR: 91% (down from 93%)
- Magic Number: 0.7 (vs. 1.1 a year ago)
- Rule of 40: 41 (22 growth + 19 margin)

**CFO commentary:** Margin expansion driven by hiring freeze in G&A and renegotiated AWS commit (~$3.2M annualized savings). Sales productivity per quota-carrying rep down 18% YoY; we have not backfilled 11 of 14 rep departures. Forecast for Q4 assumes flat net new ARR. FY26 plan in draft assumes 18–24% growth band; Street consensus is 26%.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 2 — Strata Workflow Acquisition: 9-Month Integration Review
sourceDocuments.body:
**Acquisition:** Strata Workflow, closed 9 months ago for $84M ($61M cash, $23M stock + earnout).

**Original thesis:** Add workflow automation layer; cross-sell to Meridian's 1,400 analytics customers; $18M ARR contribution Year 1, $32M Year 2.

**Actual:**
- Strata standalone ARR: $14.2M (vs. $16M plan); 6% logo churn since close (vs. 4% historical)
- Cross-sell bookings: $2.1M (vs. $7M plan)
- Platform integration: 60% complete; original 6-month timeline now tracking 14 months
- Strata CEO departed in month 5; eng head departed last month
- Earnout ($14M) tracking to NOT pay out

**Integration lead notes:** "Sales motions are genuinely different — Strata sold to ops leaders, we sell to analytics VPs. Reps are confused. Recommend separate overlay team for FY26, ~$4.5M incremental cost." CFO has flagged potential goodwill impairment review at year-end.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 3 — Competitive Intelligence Brief: Lumenpath Entry
sourceDocuments.body:
**Prepared by:** VP Strategy, for Risk & Strategy Committee

Lumenpath (Series D, $220M raised in May at $1.4B valuation) launched a direct competitor to Meridian's core analytics product in July. Pricing observations from 6 lost deals and 2 win-backs:

- List price ~35% below Meridian equivalent SKU
- Offering 18-month ramped contracts with first 6 months free
- Targeting mid-market ($1M–$10M ARR customers) — our most price-sensitive segment
- Feature parity ~70%; weaker on governance & enterprise SSO; stronger on embedded analytics

**Pipeline impact (last 90 days):**
- Mid-market win rate: 34% (was 47%)
- Average mid-market ACV: $58K (was $71K) — discounting up
- 4 of top 20 expansion opportunities now competitive vs. Lumenpath

**VP Strategy assessment:** "They are buying market share with venture dollars. Burn rate likely $80M+/yr. Window of pressure: 12–24 months until they raise again or rationalize."

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 4 — CEO Strategy Memo (Draft, for Board Pre-Read)
sourceDocuments.body:
**From:** CEO  
**Re:** FY26 Strategic Direction — "Platform Meridian"

I recommend the board endorse a three-year shift from analytics point product to **unified analytics + workflow platform**, anchored on the Strata integration and a new AI agent layer.

**Proposal:**
1. Hold operating margin at 18–20% in FY26 (no margin compression)
2. Reallocate $12M from mid-market sales into platform R&D and enterprise GTM
3. Concede some mid-market share to Lumenpath; pivot ICP upmarket ($10M+ ARR accounts)
4. Announce Platform vision at user conference in March; Street narrative shifts from "analytics vendor" to "analytics platform"
5. Initiate share buyback ($40M) to signal capital discipline

**Risks I acknowledge:** Growth could decelerate to high teens before reaccelerating in FY27. Some Strata writedown likely. Enterprise motion is unproven for us.

**What I need from the board:** Endorsement of margin floor and the upmarket pivot before I socialize with Street.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 5 — Director Dissent Memo (from two board members)
sourceDocuments.body:
**From:** Director A (growth investor seat) and Director B (former operator seat)  
**To:** Lead Director, Independent Directors

We do not support the CEO's draft as written. Three concerns:

1. **Defending margin in a competitive attack is historically a losing posture.** Lumenpath has 12–24 months of cheap capital. Conceding mid-market hands them a reference base that funds their enterprise move into our turf in FY27.

2. **The buyback is premature.** $40M deployed into buyback at current multiple returns ~5% IRR. Same capital into 25 additional AEs at current (degraded) productivity still returns 2.0x LTV/CAC.

3. **The platform narrative is being used to mask the Strata integration failure.** We should separately decide: (a) is Strata salvageable, (b) is the platform thesis correct, (c) what is the right competitive response. The CEO has bundled all three.

**Counter-proposal:** Compress margin to 12–14% in FY26, fund mid-market defense AND platform investment, defer buyback, set explicit go/no-go gates on Strata at Q2.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 6 — Customer Advisory Board Synthesis (Q3 Sessions)
sourceDocuments.body:
**Participants:** 14 customers ($120K–$1.8M ACV); facilitated by Chief Customer Officer

**Top themes:**

- **Platform fatigue:** 9 of 14 said they are actively trying to *consolidate* analytics vendors, not adopt new platforms. "We don't want a Meridian platform; we want Meridian to work better with the platforms we already have (Snowflake, Databricks, Salesforce)."
- **AI expectations:** 11 of 14 expect embedded AI/agent features at no incremental cost within 12 months; 3 said they would switch vendors over this.
- **Pricing pressure:** 6 customers volunteered that they have been approached by Lumenpath; 2 are running active POCs.
- **Workflow (Strata) interest:** Only 3 of 14 see workflow automation as a priority from Meridian specifically; most have existing tools (Zapier, Workato, internal).
- **Enterprise sentiment:** Largest 4 customers (>$1M ACV) most positive — value governance, SSO, dedicated CSM. NPS among this cohort: 62. Mid-market NPS: 31.

sourceDocuments.title: DOC 7 — Internal Audit & Risk Register Update
sourceDocuments.body:
**Prepared by:** Head of Internal Audit, for Audit Committee (shared with Risk & Strategy)

**Elevated risks this quarter:**

1. **Goodwill impairment (Strata):** Triggering events present (departed leadership, missed plan >25%). External auditors have flagged year-end testing. Potential non-cash charge: $20–35M.

2. **Sales org attrition:** Voluntary attrition in sales 27% TTM (benchmark: 18%). Exit interviews cite "unclear ICP" and "competing with Lumenpath without tools." Two regional VPs on PIPs.

3. **Concentration:** Top 10 customers now 31% of ARR (was 24% two years ago) — drift upmarket already happening organically.

4. **Disclosure controls:** If FY26 plan assumes <20% growth, Reg FD considerations on when/how to reset Street expectations. Outside counsel recommends decision by January earnings call.

5. **Key person:** No CRO; CEO running sales directly for 7 months. Search firm engaged but no finalists.

**Audit Chair recommendation:** Board should formally document scenario assumptions underlying FY26 guide before earnings call.

question: Draft a board-ready scenario planning memo defining three coherent FY26 scenarios (e.g., Defend-and-Platform, Reinvest-and-Compete, Focused-Retrenchment), with leading indicators, capital allocation implications, Strata go/no-go gates, and the governance guardrails and decision triggers the board should impose on management before the January earnings call.
